brief.prior: Two complementary dialects extending the prior batch's axis coverage. Each slot must headline at least one row-limit syntax variant the prior batch did NOT ship cleanly (e.g. TOP N, HEAD N, FETCH FIRST...ROWS ONLY, FIRST N rows). The other headline axes should pick from gaps the prior 8 dialects missed: double-quoted string literals, sole-<> not-equal spelling, double-quoted identifiers with case-fold, MINUS keyword for EXCEPT, UNIQUE keyword for DISTINCT. Avoid: MOD infix (already 4 dialects), <=> null-safe-eq (already 2), MOD function (already 2), ^= neq (already 2), || concat with typed coercion (already 2), bracket quoting (already 2), backtick quoting (already 3), CONCAT-only (already 3), SAMPLE N (already 1), at-sign wildcard (already 1), star/dot/question wildcard (already 3), EXCEPT/INTERSECT tighter precedence (already 4). Cards must be self-consistent: every example in the rendered card must parse against the final grammar — the card-conformance gate will reject otherwise.
brief.style_notes: Design exactly two dialects that are moderate in overall complexity—each should feel like a plausible real-world engine, not a puzzle. Dialect A must headline a FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY limit syntax and pair it with at least one of: MINUS for EXCEPT, sole <> not-equal, or UNIQUE for DISTINCT. Dialect B must headline either TOP N or FIRST N limit syntax and pair it with double-quoted string literals and at least one of the remaining gap axes (double-quoted identifiers with upper-case fold, MINUS, UNIQUE, sole <>). Avoid reintroducing any of the explicitly over-represented knobs listed in the prior (MOD infix, <=> null-safe eq, ^= neq, bracket quoting, backtick quoting, CONCAT-only, SAMPLE N, at-sign wildcard, star/dot/question wildcard, EXCEPT/INTERSECT tighter precedence, || with typed coercion). Every example snippet embedded in the card must be parseable under the dialect's own grammar rules—double-check that string-literal quoting, identifier quoting, and limit clauses are internally consistent.
suggested_axes: FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY row-limit (DB2/SQL:2008 style) vs TOP N (SQL Server style)
Double-quoted string literals (like MySQL sql_mode or SQLite)
Sole <> as the only not-equal spelling (no != allowed)
MINUS keyword as synonym/replacement for EXCEPT (Oracle style)
UNIQUE keyword as synonym/replacement for DISTINCT (Informix/early SQL style)
Double-quoted identifiers with automatic upper-case fold (Oracle/DB2 behavior)
FIRST N rows limit syntax (Informix/Firebird style)
|| as pure string concat with NO implicit type coercion (operands must already be strings)

config.prior: Two complementary dialects extending the prior batch's axis coverage. Each slot must headline at least one row-limit syntax variant the prior batch did NOT ship cleanly (e.g. TOP N, HEAD N, FETCH FIRST...ROWS ONLY, FIRST N rows). The other headline axes should pick from gaps the prior 8 dialects missed: double-quoted string literals, sole-<> not-equal spelling, double-quoted identifiers with case-fold, MINUS keyword for EXCEPT, UNIQUE keyword for DISTINCT. Avoid: MOD infix (already 4 dialects), <=> null-safe-eq (already 2), MOD function (already 2), ^= neq (already 2), || concat with typed coercion (already 2), bracket quoting (already 2), backtick quoting (already 3), CONCAT-only (already 3), SAMPLE N (already 1), at-sign wildcard (already 1), star/dot/question wildcard (already 3), EXCEPT/INTERSECT tighter precedence (already 4). Cards must be self-consistent: every example in the rendered card must parse against the final grammar — the card-conformance gate will reject otherwise.

ledger.description: DB2/Oracle-inspired dialect with FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY limits, MINUS for EXCEPT, UNIQUE for DISTINCT, and sole <> not-equal spelling.
ledger.name: db2_oracle_fetchfirst
primary_axes: FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY limit syntax
MINUS for EXCEPT + UNIQUE for DISTINCT

spec.description: DB2/Oracle-inspired moderate dialect: FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY limits, MINUS for EXCEPT, sole <> not-equal, UNIQUE for DISTINCT, upper-case fold with double-quoted identifiers.
spec.name: db2_oracle_fetchfirst
spec.notes: Moderate dialect A for the campaign. Headlines FETCH FIRST N ROWS ONLY (offset_fetch limit_syntax), MINUS replacing EXCEPT, sole <> as the only not-equal operator, and UNIQUE replacing DISTINCT. Upper-case identifier fold with case-sensitive double-quoted identifiers mirrors Oracle/DB2 behavior. || is pure string concat with no implicit coercion (operands must be strings). Requires semicolon terminator.

ledger.description: Informix/Firebird-inspired dialect with FIRST N limit syntax, double-quoted string literals, MINUS for EXCEPT, UNIQUE for DISTINCT, upper-case identifier fold, and sole <> not-equal.
ledger.name: informix_firebird_firstn
primary_axes: double-quoted string literals + upper-case fold identifiers
MINUS for EXCEPT + UNIQUE for DISTINCT + sole <>

spec.description: Informix/Firebird-inspired dialect with FIRST N limit syntax, double-quoted string literals, upper-case-folded double-quoted identifiers, MINUS for EXCEPT, and sole <> not-equal.
spec.name: informix_firebird_firstn
spec.notes: Headline axes: FIRST N (head_n) limit syntax from Informix/Firebird tradition, double-quoted string literals, upper-case-folded double-quoted identifiers, MINUS for EXCEPT, UNIQUE for DISTINCT, and sole <> not-equal. The || concat is pure string-only (no implicit type coercion). Double-quote serves dual duty for strings and identifiers, resolved by parser context (identifier quoting uses double quotes around identifiers in FROM/JOIN/GROUP BY positions; string literals use double quotes in value positions).
